expectations.text: A PRD file exists in outputs with RACI in the filename
expectations.evidence: File prd-raci-matrix.md exists in the outputs directory and contains 'raci' in the filename.

expectations.text: PRD contains a Problem Statement section
expectations.evidence: Line 10: '## 1. Problem Statement' section exists, describing that the RACI ProcessType is defined but has no dedicated rendering.

expectations.text: PRD contains testable acceptance criteria with checkbox items
expectations.evidence: The PRD contains a 'User Stories' table (section 4) with acceptance criteria embedded in a table column, but these are not formatted as checkbox items (- [ ]). No standalone acceptance criteria section with checkboxes exists.

expectations.text: PRD breaks work into numbered implementation phases
expectations.evidence: The PRD itself does not contain implementation phases. There is a separate implementation-roadmap.md file that breaks work into Phase 1-4, but the PRD document itself has no phased implementation section. The roadmap file uses 'Phase 1: Foundation', 'Phase 2: Editing', etc. but these are in a separate file, not in the PRD.

expectations.text: PRD explicitly defines what is out of scope
expectations.evidence: Lines 33-35: '## 3. Non-Goals' section listing 3 items: converting swimlane to RACI automatically, multi-level RACI (RASCI), and real-time collaborative editing.

expectations.text: A ROADMAP.md file exists in outputs
expectations.evidence: No file named ROADMAP.md exists. The file is named 'implementation-roadmap.md' which is a roadmap-like document but does not match the expected ROADMAP.md filename.

expectations.text: Roadmap references or links to the PRD document
expectations.evidence: The implementation-roadmap.md file does not contain any reference or link to prd-raci-matrix.md. It is a standalone implementation plan.

expectations.text: Discovery or clarifying questions were asked before writing the PRD
expectations.evidence: The PRD contains an 'Open Questions' section (section 11) at the end, but these are post-hoc questions for future consideration, not discovery questions asked to the user before writing. No transcript or evidence of pre-PRD discovery exists.
